steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The text describes Barragán's career as evolving through distinct phases, with his 1930s travels triggering a shift from his earlier style to a more modernist approach. The correct answer must reflect this three-part structure: general overview, transition trigger, and evidence of the shift.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — It summarizes the career of a particular architect, states how a journey influenced that architect's career, and then emphasizes how influential that architect's career has been.: frames the passage around who influenced Barragán rather than around the phases of his career. ✗
  • Choice B — It provides a general statement about the career of a particular architect, highlights a transition in that career, and then expounds on the ways that transition is evident in the architect's work.: the first sentence gives the career overview, the second identifies the travel-triggered shift, and the third shows how the change appears in his work. ✓
  • Choice C — It explains the design aesthetic of a particular architect, acknowledges the sources of those design choices, and then contrasts that aesthetic with the designs of earlier architects.: introduces figures not discussed in the text, misrepresenting the focus. ✗
  • Choice D — It presents a general claim about a particular architect's career, describes the early stages of the architect's career, and then explains a later shift in that career.: treats "early stages" and the transition as disconnected rather than causally linked. ✗
B is correct.
